nodes.label: AI as collaborative conversational partner
nodes.label_negative: AI as autonomous delegator for communication
nodes.grounding_span: I definitely am more likely to collaborate. I can't think of any instances where I purely delegate a task to AI.

nodes.label: AI as emotional support in difficult situations
nodes.label_negative: AI as emotionally uninformed or detached
nodes.grounding_span: it was helpful to have a third party helping me write without all of the emotions and anxiety I had wrapped up in the situation

nodes.label: AI as brainstorming and idea generator
nodes.label_negative: AI as sole creator without human input
nodes.grounding_span: using AI to brainstorm based on the role and the tutor resume has led me to have more dynamic and interesting interviews

nodes.label: Trust in AI for high-stakes tasks (billing)
nodes.label_negative: Distrust in AI for high-stakes tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I just don't trust the models to handle billing since it's pretty high-stakes.

nodes.label: AI delegation for simple tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI requiring human input for routine tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I would love to be able to fully delegate routine administrative tasks rather than needing my input as much.

nodes.label: Authentic human connection and trust
nodes.grounding_span: I don't think an AI could ever convince a client to work with us over the phone like I do and bring in that human connection

nodes.label: Professionalism and emotional regulation
nodes.grounding_span: it helped me stay professional in response to a tough situation

nodes.label: Optimism about future AI delegation
